name: MCP Manager Control

meta.description: Manage json-mcp-manager via MCP — list, start, stop, restart instances, view logs, upload tools, edit code and more. Security levels are enforced server-side.

manifest.title: MCP Manager Control
manifest.author: Torsten Feustel
manifest.description: Full management of json-mcp-manager via MCP. Respects server-side security levels.

specs.name: list_instances
specs.description: List all MCP server instances with their status, port and URL. [Valve: allow_list_instances]

specs.name: get_instance
specs.description: Get status and details of a specific MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_get_instance]

specs.name: start_instance
specs.description: Start an MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_start]

specs.name: stop_instance
specs.description: Stop a running MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_stop]

specs.name: restart_instance
specs.description: Restart an MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_restart]

specs.name: reinstall_instance
specs.description: Reinstall dependencies for an MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_reinstall]

specs.name: get_install_log
specs.description: Get the installation log for an MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_install_log]

specs.name: get_runtime_log
specs.description: Get the runtime log for an MCP server instance. [Valve: allow_runtime_log]

specs.name: get_instance_config
specs.description: Get the full configuration of an MCP server instance including values. [Valve: allow_get_config]

specs.name: get_settings
specs.description: Get current manager settings: auth status, edit mode, host, port and MCP token status. [Valve: allow_get_settings]

specs.name: get_tool_code
specs.description: Get the Python source code of an MCP server tool. Requires full edit mode. [Valve: allow_get_code]

specs.name: save_tool_code
specs.description: Save updated Python source code for an MCP server tool. Requires full edit mode. [Valve: allow_save_code]

specs.name: validate_tool_code
specs.description: Validate Python tool code: syntax check, runtime inspection and schema generation. Requires full edit mode. [Valve: allow_validate_code]

code.description: The Python source code to validate

specs.name: export_tool
specs.description: Export an MCP server tool as OpenWebUI-compatible JSON. Requires full edit mode. [Valve: allow_export_tool]

specs.name: delete_instance
specs.description: Delete an MCP server instance and its config. Requires upload or full edit mode. [Valve: allow_delete]

specs.name: get_tool_template
specs.description: Returns the Python code template AND the complete JSON upload format. Always call this before writing or uploading a new tool — it shows exactly what the upload_tool() JSON must look like. [Valve: allow_get_template]

specs.name: upload_tool
specs.description: Upload and install a new tool. json_content must be a JSON array (not object) with one element containing: id (snake_case), user_id ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'), name, meta (description + manifest), specs (list of tool definitions), content (Python source code string). Call get_tool_template() first to see the exact format. Requires upload or full edit mode. [Valve: allow_upload]

json_content.description: The complete tool JSON as a string — must be a JSON array. Call get_tool_template() to see the required format.
